item.title: Monitor Claude Code Usage With Grafana
item.description: Use Grafana Cloud and OpenTelemetry to observe your Claude token usage.

item.title: DNS is Simple. DNS is Hard.
item.description: DNS looks like configuration, but behaves like a global control plane with weak consistency and hidden state

item.title: The blurred line between overengineering and anticipating change
item.description: Check out Giorgio Garofalo's blog post

item.title: The Trouble with Fusion: Nuclear Fusion Will Not Give Us Infinite Power
item.description: Fusion will not be built for a long time. There is an astounding and impressive amount of work being done, much of it useful, but none of it is even in the same league as a commercial power plant, not to mention ballparks. As mentioned above, only a single facility has ever reached a net-positive amount of energy production. Saying that you will soon be building power plants is like keeping a campfire lit in the rain using a flamethrower and then saying you are going to build a coal power plant using your patented 24/7-flamethrower-method.

item.title: Package Upgrades Feel Like Russian Roulette
item.description: Axios just got compromised. 83M weekly downloads. A RAT on every machine. Here's what shipping desktop software taught us about npm supply chain risk.

item.title: Craft is Untouchable - Christopher Butler
item.description: AI doesn't threaten craft—the temptation to skip iteration does.

item.title: Will everything become an API?
item.description: How do you value a business when the specter of a new model looms over EBITDA multiples and forward ARR?

item.title: your hex editor should color-code bytes
item.description: alice pellerin • 2026-03-31
item.author: (https://simonomi.dev)

item.title: Building Multi-Tenant SaaS with Rails 8, Caddy, and Kamal
item.description: A production guide to building multi-tenant SaaS with Rails 8 — subdomain routing, custom domains, and automatic SSL with Caddy's on-demand TLS.
item.author: (Mooktakim Ahmed)

item.title: Introducing Positron Flux
item.description: Positron Flux is a new a delivery excellence workbench

item.title: My letter to my reps regarding California’s age verification law
item.description: My name’s Chris Taylor. I’m a software engineer and a constituent in your district. I grew up in California and got a computer engineering degree from UCSB (the one by the beach). I’ve worked on defense robotics, drones, and other high-stakes industrial systems (trains, forklift trucks), with experience in cybersecurity and AI.

item.title: Liquid Glass updates in 26.4
item.description: Apple recently updated all their OSs to 26.4, and there were plenty of Liquid Glass updates. Most of them for the better! Here are some of the changes that stood out to me, as well as a bit more grumbling about Liquid Glass in general (sorry).

item.title: Code and Cake - Your job isn't programming
item.description: We’ve been doing software development wrong. When trying to improve a poor codebase, the solutions we often reach to are generic and technical. Move the frontend to React. Split the backend into microservices. Rewrite everything in Rust. Some of these approaches can have real benefits in the right situation, but none of them are going to solve the core problems with bad codebases.

item.title: Every table should be a spreadsheet
item.description: Building spreadsheet-style superpowers for native HTML tables.

item.title: Your Engineers Aren't Lazy, Your Codebase Is Punishing Them
item.description: A two-minute interactive audit to score whether technical debt is dragging your engineering team. Five signals that separate people problems from code problems.
item.author: (Ally Piechowski)

item.title: A Dot a Day Keeps the Clutter Away — Scott Lawson
item.description: The simplest organization system I've tried is a sheet of colored dot stickers. It's also the best.

item.title: Why I Like Real Estate
item.description: January 2023 I’m often asked why I like real estate better than any other asset class like equities, bonds, crypto, startup shares etc. Beyond that I think the returns are better, every time I’m asked…

item.title: An effect notation based on with-clauses and blocks
item.description: A bad habit of mine is that I’ll write 10.000 or 20.000 words on a topic, and then never publish that. A lot of my longer-form writing never sees the light of day because it gets stuck in editing purgatory. Editing small pieces is easy and quick, editing long pieces is not. I’ve been wanting to write more about effect notations for a while now and have been noodling on something I hope people might be alright with. At a minimum wouldn't mind seeing pop up in code.

item.title: The World's First Bullshit
item.description:
I opened Twitter this morning and three different startups were announcing "the world's first" something. An AI CMO, an autonomous AI marketer, and a design agent "with taste," which is a phrase that made me close my laptop for about ten minutes.
None of them are the world's first anything.

item.title: Minimum Release Age is an Underrated Supply Chain Defense | Dani Akash
item.description: A 7-day package delay would have blocked installs in most short-lived malicious publish attacks from the last 8 years

item.title: I Read Claude Code's Leaked Source. Here's What's Inside.
item.description: 380K lines of TypeScript leaked via source maps. Undercover mode, autonomous agents, and a Tamagotchi.
item.author: (Saad Naveed)

item.description: This was written on March 1, 2026

item.title: I Read the Leaked Claude Code Source — Here’s What I Found
item.description: Today, the source code for Claude Code — Anthropic’s AI-powered CLI tool — leaked online. Naturally, I did what any curious developer would do.

item.title: Human.json
item.description: human.json is an interesting idea, but comes with the usual semantic web problems.

item.title: When to Use an Agent
item.description: There are times where it is prudent to use an AI agent (like Codex) aggressively. There are other times where they should be avoided like the plague. The question of into which bucket a particular endeavor falls is difficult to answer.

item.title: The Thought Factory
item.description: Software factories are not abstract enough.

item.title: Tracing Goroutines in Realtime with eBPF — Ozan Sazak
item.description: Built a Go runtime tracer called xgotop to trace and visualize goroutine lifecycles and memory allocations in realtime, and won eBPF Summit '25 Hackathon with it!

item.title: When an ALB Can Replace Nginx (and When It Can't) — yaw
item.description: An AWS ALB handles routing, SSL, health checks, and redirects natively. Here's when you can drop Nginx entirely.

item.description: Everything laid out in JS. Resize horizontally and vertically, then click the logos.

item.description: This is a post about “vibe coding”/”agentic engineering”, and my mental journey to come to terms with it.

item.description: OkCupid and Match settle with Trump FTC, don't have to pay any financial penalty.

item.title: Copilot - Terms of Use
Supply Chain Attack on Axios Pulls Malicious Dependency from...
item.description: A supply chain attack on Axios introduced a malicious dependency, plain-crypto-js@4.2.1, published minutes earlier and absent from the project’s GitHu...

item.title: .NET CLI Shebangs and Argument Parsing | no dogma blog
item.description: Trying to pass something like -v or -h to a .NET CLI application that uses the shebang style of `dotnet run app.cs` can cause issues because `dotnet run` itself has options that conflict with the application's arguments. Here's how to work around that.

item.title: Prediction: The Shopify CEO's Pull Request Will Never Be Merged Nor Closed
item.description: The CEO of Shopify used an AI tool called autoresearch to optimize Liquid parsing speed. The code quality is poor and no one will ever merge it.

item.title: axios Compromised: npm Supply Chain Attack via Dependency Injection
item.description: axios 1.14.1 was published to npm via a compromised maintainer account, injecting a trojanized dependency that executes a multi-platform reverse shell on install. No source code changes in axios itself, just a new entry in package.json.

item.title: GitHub backs down, kills Copilot PR ‘tips’ after backlash
item.description: Updated: Letting Copilot alter others' PRs was the wrong judgment call, says product manager

item.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview · Ollama Blog
item.description: Today, we're previewing the fastest way to run Ollama on Apple silicon, powered by MLX, Apple's machine learning framework.

item.title: Android developer verification: Rolling out to all developers on Play Console and Android Developer Console
item.description: News and insights on the Android platform, developer tools, and events.

item.title: Artemis II Is Not Safe to Fly (Idle Words)
GitHub - drona23/claude-token-efficient: Universal CLAUDE.md - cut Claude output tokens by 63%. Drop-in. No code changes.

item.title: Turning a MacBook into a Touchscreen with $1 of Hardware
item.description: We turned a MacBook into a touchscreen using only $1 of hardware and a little bit of computer vision.

item.title: axios Compromised on npm - Malicious Versions Drop Remote Access Trojan - StepSecurity
item.description: Hijacked maintainer account used to publish poisoned axios releases including 1.14.1 and 0.30.4. The attacker injected a hidden dependency that drops a cross platform RAT. We are actively investigating and will update this post with a full technical analysis.

item.title: Notes on going solo: celebrating 6 years of Studio Self
item.description: Since roughly // broadly 2020, I’ve been running a solo-powered minor empire. I have no employees, and my only office is my home office, filled as it is with cat hair and various comic books. My business is: me, a laptop, a set of AI tools that scale the parts

item.title: You probably don't need to lift state - Matt Smith
item.description: A quick React tip: don't lift state by default. Keep it close to where it's used unless you actually need to share it.
item.author: (https://allthingssmitty.com/about/)

item.title: Who's Teaching the Juniors?
item.description: Companies are replacing juniors with AI and trusting seniors to handle the rest. But they've forgotten that every senior was once a junior who learned by doing.

item.title: Good CTE, bad CTE
item.description: The planner treats CTEs very differently depending on how you write them. Here's what happens under the hood, version by version, through PostgreSQL 18.

item.title: Getting a Job in 2026
item.description: The job market is tough right now in tech. You hear about it everywhere. Whether it's due to AI, over-hiring due to ZIRP, or *waves hands broadly* the economy, getting a job is a journey these days. This is how it went for me. I…

item.title: Agentic developer experience starts with your system, not your prompts
item.description: The new 'time to hello world' isn't determined by a developer reading your getting started guide. It's someone typing a naive prompt into an agent. How should we think about that?

item.title: Your ticket is a prompt
item.description: The instinct to break work into atomic tickets was right for human teams. For agents, it reproduces the same fragmentation disease at machine speed.

item.title: Office, messaging and verbs — Benedict Evans
item.description: Mainframes replaced adding machine, PCs replaced mainframes, and now the web and mobile are replacing PCs. With each of these changes, we started by making the new thing fit into the old way of getting our work done. but over time, we change the work to fit the new tool.

item.title: Every Package You Install Can Read Your Secrets
item.description: Why npm, pip, and direct Git dependencies can expose your secrets, how the attack works, and which controls actually reduce the blast radius.
item.author: (Eliran Turgeman)

item.title: Moving the Critic Into My Editor
item.description: What if you could have an AI critic semantically linting your writing, inline?
item.author: (https://www.joshbeckman.org/about/)

item.title: Speech to Text on WSL in one line
item.description:
Let's assume you downloaded a Voice file from your iPhone and now want to convert it to a text. You just need to run that line: Full command
bash MODEL=...

item.description: K = 0.728. Nikolai Kardashev designed the scale in 1964 to classify civilizations by how much energy they command. Type I harnesses all the energy hitting its p

item.title: Why Don’t You Use String Views (as std::wstring_view) Instead of Passing std::wstring by const&?

item.title: How to Survive in Tech in 2026
item.description: Human leverage in the AI era - business acumen, cross-functional teams, daily AI practice, real-world connections, and deep curiosity beyond the hype.
